title: Mathematics   31 4. Mark the points as A, B, C and D, where the circle cuts the   x -axis and   y -axis,
subject: Mathematics
unit: Unit L - the other end to move freely along the circle
activityTitle: Mathematics   31 4. Mark the points as A, B, C and D, where the circle cuts the   x -axis and   y -axis,
objective:
To prepare a model to illustrate the
values of sine function and cosine
function for different angles which are
multiples of   π   and   π
2   .
A   stand   fitted   with   0º-360º
protractor and a circular plastic
sheet fixed with handle which can
be rotated at the centre of the
protractor.
procedure:
1. Take a stand fitted with 0º-360º   protractor.
2. Consider the radius of protractor as 1 unit.
observation:
1. Angle made by the needle in one complete revolution is _________.
2.   π
cos  6   =   _________ = cos   6
π    
−    
   
π
sin  6   =   _________   sin (2   ______). =   π +
3. sine function is non-negative in _______ and _______ quadrants.
4. cosine function is non-negative in ______ and _____ quadrants.
discussion:
1. The activity can be used to get the values for tan, cot, sec, and cosec functions
also.
2. From this activity students may learn that
sin (–   θ ) = – sin   θ   and   cos (–   θ ) = – cos   θ
This activity can be applied to other trigonometric functions also.
